title: Newswire mobile search and discovery across markets, companies, topics, and countries
productContext: Fictional global finance-news mobile app (product name 'Newswire') for iOS and Android. Search is where a researcher, trend-follower, or new user goes to discover coverage across markets, companies, topics, and countries. Results mix publisher articles with an optional machine-generated AI summary and machine-assembled discovery groupings (trending tickers, related topics, country hubs). Newswire is reader-and-aggregation software only; it does not employ analysts, does not set a relevance authority, and the ranking of results is an inferred relevance signal, not an objective truth ranking. The app is pre-scale: there is no published index size, no coverage metric, and no AI-accuracy figure, and any trend signal shown is an inferred observation, not a forecast. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: researcher running a precise query across markets, companies, and countries and needing filters and source attribution on every result
trend-follower exploring what is rising in attention across topics or tickers, aware that 'trending' is an inferred signal not a forecast
new user exploring the app for the first time via search with no saved topics or follow list
jobs: search across markets, companies, topics, and countries and filter results without losing publisher attribution on any item
discover related topics, trending tickers, and country hubs, knowing these are machine-assembled and inferred, not editorial picks or forecasts
tell apart a publisher article result from a machine-generated AI summary result at a glance in the result list
recover gracefully from a typo, a zero-results query, or a slow query without being shown fabricated totals or accuracy claims
requiredJourneys: researcher types a precise query, applies company and country filters, and scans results where every item shows its publisher, timestamp, and AI-summary marker
trend-follower opens discovery, scans trending tickers and related topics, and confirms the screen labels them inferred, not forecast or authoritative
new user with no follow list runs an exploratory query, gets a useful starting set and discovery suggestions, and can refine without signing up first
any user hits a zero-results or slow-query state and gets a recoverable path (suggestions, filters reset, retry) with no fabricated result totals
constraints: Do not present search ranking as an objective truth or relevance-authority ranking; result order is an inferred relevance signal and the UI must not claim one publisher or result is objectively more correct than another.

Any AI summary in results must be labelled machine-generated and source-attributed; do not present it as the publisher's verdict, Newswire's editorial pick, or a source of record, and AI-summary results must be visually distinct from publisher-article results.
Do not display any AI-accuracy percentage, confidence score, coverage metric, or index-size figure; the app is pre-scale and no such numbers are supplied.

Trending tickers, related topics, and country hubs must be labelled machine-assembled and inferred; do not present them as forecasts, investment signals, buy/sell recommendations, or editorial endorsements.
Every search result — publisher article or AI summary — must keep a publisher label and timestamp visible in the list; filtering by market, company, topic, or country must never strip attribution.

Zero-results, typo-correction, and slow-query states must be honest and recoverable: the UI must not fabricate result totals, pad with synthetic results, or claim a slow query returned more than it did.
Discovery for a new user must not require sign-up or a saved follow list; an exploratory query must return a usable starting set and discovery suggestions without gating.
The product name Newswire is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, rating agency, or data vendor.

mobileRules: the search field sits in the thumb zone, keeps the active filter chips visible above the keyboard, and the submit affordance stays reachable in portrait
the query-slow state surfaces an honest 'still searching' indicator and offers a refine-or-cancel path without claiming results have arrived
every result row keeps its publisher label, timestamp, and AI-summary marker visible when filters collapse the list on a narrow viewport
the zero-results and results-loading-error states keep filters and recent queries reachable so the user can recover without retyping
trending tickers, related topics, and country hubs each carry a 'machine-assembled, inferred' label wherever they appear
selecting a country hub filters results without implying Newswire ranks that market's sources by authority
